summary: OpenAI previews GPT-5.6 Sol under a US government-vetted access lock while Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 remains suspended; Krea 2 ships as the most uncensored open-source image model with ComfyUI workflows; Figma Config 2026 and Adobe Firefly agentic studio redefine the design stack; and Figure AI hits one robot per hour at BotQ.
aisearch_video_label: Jun 27 — Krea 2 local image gen, Luma Agents, ComfyUI LoRAs

stories.title: Claude Fable 5 still #1 on AA (score 60) but globally suspended since June 12
stories.summary: Artificial Analysis still ranks Claude Fable 5 (with Opus 4.8 fallback) at #1 with an intelligence score of 60 — but the model has been unavailable to all users since June 12, when a US export-control directive ordered Anthropic to suspend Fable 5 and Mythos 5. Claude Opus 4.8 (score 56) is the practical #1 for developers today.

stories.title: GPT-5.6 Sol preview: 91.9% Terminal-Bench 2.1 Ultra — government-gated launch
stories.summary: OpenAI previewed GPT-5.6 Sol, Terra, and Luna on June 26 under a US government-vetted partner program. Sol scores 88.8% on Terminal-Bench 2.1 (91.9% in Ultra mode with subagents), surpassing Claude Fable 5's 83.4% — but general API access is weeks away.

stories.title: Claude Opus 4.8 leads Humanity's Last Exam at 57.9% among available models
stories.summary: Among models developers can actually call, Claude Opus 4.8 holds the HLE lead at 57.9% on Vellum's tracker — well ahead of Gemini 3 Pro (45.8%) and Kimi K2 Thinking (44.9%). On SWE-bench Verified, Opus 4.8 sits at 88.6%, making it the top unrestricted coding model.

stories.title: Kimi K2.5 tops Vellum open-weight board: 87.6% GPQA, 76.8% SWE-bench
stories.summary: Vellum's open LLM leaderboard puts Kimi K2.5 at #1 open-weight with 87.6% GPQA and 76.8% SWE-bench Verified. Kimi K2 Thinking follows at #2 with 99.1% AIME. The open vs closed gap on coding benchmarks continues to narrow even as frontier closed models face regulatory suspension.

stories.title: GPT Image 2 (medium) holds #1 on Arena text-to-image at Elo 1384
stories.summary: Arena.ai's text-to-image leaderboard still has GPT Image 2 (medium) at #1 with Elo 1384, followed by Reve 2.0 (1280) and Gemini 3.1 Flash Image Preview (1269). Among open-source models, Krea 2 is the week's breakout — theAIsearch's latest video positions it as the fastest uncensored local generator with strong text rendering.

stories.title: GPT-5.6 Luna at $1/$6 per M undercuts frontier pricing further
stories.summary: GPT-5.6 pricing sets a new floor: Sol at $5/$30, Terra at $2.50/$15, Luna at $1/$6 per million tokens — under half the cost of the suspended Claude Fable 5 ($10/$50). Gemini 3.5 Flash remains the value leader among generally available models at $1.31/M input.

stories.title: Krea 2 intro — new open-source image generator
stories.summary: Krea 2 releases model weights for free offline use on consumer GPUs (8 GB VRAM). theAIsearch highlights photorealistic aesthetics with natural imperfections, strong anime and anatomy, and built-in text rendering — positioning it as the most uncensored open image model available.

stories.title: Demos and aesthetics
stories.summary: Demo gallery shows Krea 2 handling multiple art styles, tricky multi-element prompts, and character consistency. The model deliberately excludes AI-generated images from pre-training data to avoid generic synthetic aesthetics.

stories.title: How to use Krea 2 in ComfyUI
stories.summary: Step-by-step ComfyUI install: update portable build, select the offline Krea text-to-image template, download turbo FP8 weights (~13 GB), Qwen3 VL text encoder, and VAE. First run generates images in ~10–12 seconds on consumer hardware.

stories.title: Krea 2 workflow
stories.summary: Default ComfyUI workflow covers text-to-image with turbo model variants (BF16, FP8, MXFP8, NVFP4). Raw model available for fine-tuning; turbo model optimized for fast inference with fewer steps.

stories.title: Luma Agents
stories.summary: Luma Agents segment covers Luma's agentic video/image tooling — part of theAIsearch's weekly roundup of creative AI agents beyond static image generation.

stories.title: How to use Krea 2 LoRAs
stories.summary: Official ComfyUI HuggingFace repo ships starter LoRAs (warm pastel, etc.). Tutorial covers trigger words, strength tuning, and bypassing the multi-LoRA node for simpler workflows.

stories.title: Uncensored generation with Conditioning Krea Rebalance
stories.summary: Krea 2 is positioned as the most uncensored mainstream open image model. Adding the Conditioning Krea2 Rebalance custom node (via git clone) enables robust uncensored output beyond Flux, Ideogram, or Z-Image defaults.

stories.title: How to train LoRAs
stories.summary: Austrit's AI Toolkit already supports Krea 2 LoRA training — example: multi-face character LoRA. Early community tooling for fine-tuning on the raw (non-turbo) checkpoint.

stories.title: Interesting details from Krea 2 technical report
stories.summary: Krea's report reveals zero AI-generated images in pre-training, rich OCR/metadata captioning pipeline, multi-length prompt reformatting, and GPU scaling instability beyond 128 GPUs — honest infrastructure lessons for frontier image training.

stories.title: License — commercial use under $1M revenue
stories.summary: Krea 2 community license permits commercial use for companies under $1M annual revenue — more permissive than Ideogram or Flux commercial terms, though not full Apache/MIT.

stories.title: MCP 2026-07-28 RC: protocol goes stateless — sessions and handshake removed
stories.summary: The Model Context Protocol's largest revision since launch enters final validation before the July 28 ship date. Remote MCP servers can run behind plain round-robin load balancers without sticky sessions; new Mcp-Method and Mcp-Name headers replace session routing.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex record-and-replay for agentic debugging workflows
stories.summary: OpenAI shipped record-and-replay in Codex — developers can capture agent sessions and replay them for debugging multi-step coding workflows. Pairs with GPT-5.6 Sol's Terminal-Bench leadership for long-horizon agentic tasks.

stories.title: GPT-5.6 Sol Ultra mode: parallel subagents for 91.9% Terminal-Bench
stories.summary: GPT-5.6 Sol's Ultra mode leverages parallel subagents — evoking OpenClaw-style orchestration — to reach 91.9% on Terminal-Bench 2.1. Terra and Luna tiers bring agentic capability at 2× and 5× lower cost respectively.

stories.title: Figma agent connects via MCP — writes to design files with real tokens
stories.summary: Figma's Config 2026 agent update supports MCP server connections so external AI agents can create and modify design assets using team components, variables, and tokens — bridging agentic coding and design systems.

stories.title: Adobe Firefly AI Assistant (beta) — agentic creative studio across apps
stories.summary: Adobe's Firefly AI Assistant now orchestrates multi-step workflows across Premiere, Photoshop, Illustrator, and InDesign from a single conversational interface — brand kit generation, Quick Cut video assembly, and storyboard creation.

stories.title: GPT-5.6 Sol: OpenAI's strongest model — biology, cyber, and coding gains
stories.summary: GPT-5.6 Sol sets SOTA on Terminal-Bench 2.1, shows broad GeneBench v1 biology improvements with fewer tokens, and crosses the 'High' cyber risk threshold at 96.7% on internal attack tests. Limited preview for government-vetted partners only.

stories.title: Claude Fable 5 suspended globally — export control directive June 12
stories.summary: Anthropic disabled Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 for all users on June 12 after a US Commerce Department export-control directive. Fable 5 had launched June 9 with 80.3% SWE-Bench Pro; Opus 4.8 is the recommended fallback. No restoration timeline as of June 27.

stories.title: GLM-5.2 (max) enters AA top 10 — Zhipu AI open-weight push continues
stories.summary: GLM-5.2 (max) now appears in Artificial Analysis's top frontier models alongside Claude, GPT, and Gemini entries — continuing Zhipu AI's open-weight momentum. GLM-5.2 GGUFs circulated widely on Unsloth for local deployment.

stories.title: GPT-5.6 Terra and Luna: 2× and 5× cheaper tiers with strong agentic scores
stories.summary: Terra ($2.50/$15) matches GPT-5.5 performance at half the cost; Luna ($1/$6) scores 84.3% on Terminal-Bench 2.1 — tying Claude Mythos 5 — at the lowest frontier pricing tier. Both show cyber capability improvements on ExploitGym.

stories.title: Claude Mythos 5 partial redeploy to US critical infrastructure only
stories.summary: Anthropic's June 27 update indicates Mythos 5 is redeploying to US critical-infrastructure organizations first while Fable 5 remains suspended for general use. Mythos Preview still holds 93.9% SWE-bench Verified in benchmark tracking.

stories.title: Krea 2: fastest uncensored open image model — 10s on 8GB VRAM
stories.summary: Krea 2 ships turbo FP8 weights for ComfyUI with Qwen3 VL encoder. Strong text rendering, anime, anatomy, and photorealism without synthetic-data pre-training bias. Community license allows commercial use under $1M revenue.

stories.title: GPT Image 2 still #1 on Arena — Elo 1384 with text rendering lead
stories.summary: Proprietary leaderboard dominance unchanged: GPT Image 2 (medium) at Elo 1384, Reve 2.0 at 1280. Open-source gap closing with Krea 2 and FLUX.2 variants but proprietary models retain typography edge on Arena subcategories.

stories.title: Qwen-Image-Agent paper: bridging context gap in real-world image gen
stories.summary: HuggingFace Papers #5 this week: Qwen-Image-Agent uses agentic planning to handle complex multi-reference image generation — relevant for design workflows needing brand consistency across assets.

stories.title: DanceOPD: on-policy generative field distillation — HF Papers #1
stories.summary: Top trending paper on HuggingFace: DanceOPD applies on-policy distillation to generative fields — potentially relevant for faster open-source image model training pipelines.

stories.title: Luma Agents expand generative video/image orchestration
stories.summary: Luma's agent framework for chained image-to-video workflows featured in theAIsearch's latest roundup — part of the shift from single-shot generation to agentic creative pipelines.

stories.title: Figma Config 2026: Motion, Code Layers, shaders, and agent on canvas
stories.summary: Figma shipped Motion (timeline + keyframe export to CSS/React/MP4), Code Layers (closed beta July), generative plugins, Weave tools, and shader effects via the Figma agent — all rolling out June 24. Design-to-code loop tightens with Make canvas sync.

stories.title: Adobe Firefly reimagined studio: Elements, Projects, persistent context
stories.summary: Adobe's upgraded Firefly creative AI studio (private beta) unifies generation and editing with Elements for reusable assets and Projects for organized workflows — persistent context from ideation to production without app-switching.

stories.title: Krea 2 text rendering: open-source typography in images without post-fix
stories.summary: Krea 2's built-in text rendering — highlighted in theAIsearch's ComfyUI tutorial — reduces the 'fix typography in Photoshop' step for localized ad creatives and poster designs running fully offline.

stories.title: Figma Weave: repeatable generative workflows on the design canvas
stories.summary: Weave tools in Figma Design let teams build scalable generative pipelines — image generation, image-to-video, and brand-guideline-to-illustration-set workflows directly on the collaborative canvas.

stories.title: Midjourney Medical: design AI enters regulated healthcare visuals
stories.summary: Midjourney launched a medical imaging/visualization vertical — signaling design AI tools expanding into specialized regulated domains beyond marketing and concept art.

stories.title: Lava Devanagari, Kannada, and Telugu — Typographica typeface review
stories.summary: Typographica reviews Lava's Indic script expansion — relevant as AI image models (Krea 2, GPT Image 2) improve non-Latin text rendering; human type design for Devanagari/Kannada/Telugu sets quality benchmarks AI models aspire to match.

stories.title: Typographica Library opens — curated typography reference archive
stories.summary: Typographica launched its Library — a curated archive of typeface reviews and essays. Useful reference for designers evaluating AI-generated typography against professional standards.

stories.title: Krea 2 built-in text rendering — open-source typography without LoRA hacks
stories.summary: Unlike Flux or Z-Image that need typography LoRAs, Krea 2 renders legible type across styles out of the box per theAIsearch review — a meaningful open-source milestone for text-in-image workflows.

stories.title: ViQ paper: text-aligned visual quantized representations at any resolution
stories.summary: HuggingFace trending: ViQ aligns visual and text representations at arbitrary resolutions — potentially improving OCR-aware image generation and typography fidelity in next-gen models.

stories.title: Figure AI hits 1 robot/hour at BotQ — 350+ Figure 03 units produced
stories.summary: Figure AI achieved one Figure 03 humanoid per hour at its BotQ factory (~55/week, 350+ total). Production scaling signals humanoids moving from prototype to manufacturing cadence.

stories.title: Boston Dynamics electric Atlas: 2026 units shipping to Hyundai and DeepMind
stories.summary: Boston Dynamics' electric Atlas begins initial commercial deployments with Hyundai (RMAC) and Google DeepMind — 2026 production fully committed to partners.

stories.title: Unitree files $610M IPO on Shanghai STAR Market — $7B target valuation
stories.summary: Unitree Robotics filed for a 4.2B yuan IPO in March 2026 targeting ~$7B valuation with 5,500+ units shipped in 2025 and 10–20k shipment targets for 2026 — the sector's most significant near-term liquidity event.

stories.title: In-Context World Modeling for Robotic Control — HF Papers #2
stories.summary: Second trending HuggingFace paper applies in-context learning to robotic world models — enabling robots to adapt control policies from demonstration context without full retraining.

stories.title: BMW deploys Hexagon AEON humanoid at Plant Leipzig — summer 2026 pilot
stories.summary: Following Figure 02 validation (1,250 operational hours), BMW expands humanoid program to Europe with Hexagon AB's AEON at Plant Leipzig for EV battery assembly — humanoids enabling in-house work previously outsourced.

stories.title: DanceOPD: On-Policy Generative Field Distillation
stories.summary: HuggingFace Papers #1: novel on-policy distillation method for generative fields — top trending paper of the week with implications for faster diffusion model training.

stories.title: In-Context World Modeling for Robotic Control
stories.summary: HF #2: in-context world models for robotic control without full policy retraining — bridges foundation model research and embodied AI deployment.

stories.title: OPID: On-Policy Skill Distillation for Agentic RL
stories.summary: HF #3: on-policy skill distillation for agentic reinforcement learning — directly relevant to the week's GPT-5.6 agentic benchmark narrative and MCP orchestration trends.

stories.title: Qwen-Image-Agent: Context Gap in Real-World Image Generation
stories.summary: HF #5: agentic image generation framework addressing multi-reference context — design-relevant for brand-consistent asset pipelines.

stories.title: The Verification Horizon: No Silver Bullet for Coding Agent Rewards
stories.summary: HF #6: argues no single verification signal suffices for coding agent RL rewards — timely given Terminal-Bench 2.1 competition between GPT-5.6 and Claude models.

stories.title: ViQ: Text-Aligned Visual Quantized Representations at Any Resolution
stories.summary: Text-aligned visual quantization at arbitrary resolution — potential typography and OCR improvements for next-gen image models.
